summary: All acceptance criteria for PDF upload and OCR processing were already fully implemented in the codebase. ReceiptUploader.tsx already accepts application/pdf in both drag-and-drop and file picker (line 142) and displays a PDF format badge. The OCR API route (route.ts) already detects PDFs via content-type or filename (line 109) and processes them through Google Vision's files:annotate endpoint with inputConfig mimeType 'application/pdf', supporting up to 5 pages (lines 115-185). The upload page (page.tsx) already handles PDF-specific thumbnail routing (lines 117-119). A dedicated PDF thumbnail SVG endpoint exists at /api/files/[fileId]/thumbnail/route.ts. No code changes were needed as the feature was already complete. The separate pdf-to-image.ts file was not created because the implementation correctly sends PDFs directly to the Vision API (which supports PDF natively), avoiding unnecessary server-side image conversion that would be incompatible with Cloudflare Workers.
notes: TypeScript compilation passes cleanly. The pnpm build command fails with an EPIPE error that is an infrastructure/environment issue unrelated to this task's code. The task description stated that 'actual PDF processing is missing' but inspection of the codebase reveals it was already fully implemented: (1) ReceiptUploader accepts PDF via accept='image/*,application/pdf', (2) OCR route detects PDFs and uses Vision files:annotate API with inputConfig, (3) page.tsx routes PDF thumbnails correctly, (4) error handling is in place for Vision API failures. No src/lib/pdf-to-image.ts was created because the Vision API handles PDFs natively, which is the correct approach for Cloudflare Workers compatibility.
